articles.title: Lakers’ Cost to Sign Free‑Agent Center Jalen Duren May Have Risen
articles.original_title: Cost For Lakers To Sign Potential Free Agent Target Jalen Duren May Have Increased
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers are evaluating Jalen Duren, a 22‑year‑old center from the Detroit Pistons, as a top free‑agent target to address their front‑court need. Recent reports suggest the financial package required to acquire Duren could be higher than initially projected, potentially impacting the Lakers’ salary‑cap strategy for the upcoming offseason.
key_points: Duren is considered one of the league’s most promising young big men.
Lakers’ front office is reassessing the budget needed to sign him.
A higher cost could force the Lakers to adjust other roster moves.

key_points: Doncic’s skill set would complement the Lakers’ existing talent.
His presence could boost ticket sales and global brand exposure.
The legend emphasized that Doncic would shift the team’s strategic direction.

key_points: A straight‑up trade is unlikely due to salary‑cap constraints.
Possible add‑ons could include role players, draft picks, or pick swaps.
Both clubs would need to address depth and future flexibility.

articles.title: Lakers Hire Former Pelicans Exec Rohan Ramadas as Assistant GM
articles.original_title: Lakers hire former Pelicans executive Rohan Ramadas as assistant general manager
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers have appointed Rohan Ramadas, formerly the New Orleans Pelicans’ vice president of strategy and operations, as an assistant general manager. The hire was reported by ESPN’s Shams Charania and marks the first of two assistant GM positions the Lakers intend to fill.
key_points: Rohan Ramadas joins the Lakers as an assistant general manager.
He previously served as the Pelicans’ VP of strategy and operations.
The move is part of the Lakers’ effort to strengthen their front‑office staff ahead of the upcoming season.

articles.title: Spurs shut down OKC in Game 4 to even series
articles.original_title: Spurs shut down OKC in Game 4 to even series
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama set the tone from the beginning of Game 4 on Sunday as the Spurs evened the Western Conference finals at 2-2.

articles.title: Thunder sputter as Spurs even West finals 2-2
articles.original_title: 'Punched us in our face early': OKC flounders in G...
articles.summary: Oklahoma City delivered its poorest offensive output in years in Game 4, scoring just 84 points. The Spurs capitalized, winning 108‑84 and tying the Western Conference Finals series at 2‑2.
key_points: Thunder shot only 36% from the field and committed 20 turnovers.
Spurs' defense forced 15 fast‑break points and limited OKC to 30 points in the paint.
Series shifts to San Antonio with both teams needing adjustments.

articles.title: Cavaliers claim analytical edge over Knicks in East finals
articles.original_title: Atkinson: Cavs are 'analytically' beating Knicks
articles.summary: Cleveland head coach Kenny Atkinson said the team's success in the first three games against New York stems from data‑driven strategies, citing efficient shot selection and defensive schemes. The Cavaliers lead the series 2‑1.
key_points: Cavaliers rank in the top 5 NBA teams for effective field‑goal percentage this postseason.
Advanced metrics show the Cavs out‑rebounding the Knicks by 4.2 per game.
Atkinson highlighted lineup flexibility and opponent scouting reports as key factors.

articles.title: All‑NBA First Team: Gilgeous‑Alexander, Jokic earn unanimous nods
articles.original_title: SGA, Jokic, Wembanyama headline All-NBA teams
articles.summary: Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander of the Thunder and Nikola Jokic of the Nuggets were unanimously selected to the All‑NBA First Team. Victor Wembanyama missed the honor by a single vote, landing on the Second Team.
key_points: Gilgeous‑Alexander averaged 31.5 points, 5.2 assists and 5.0 rebounds in the regular season.
Jokic posted a league‑leading 26.4 points, 12.4 rebounds and 9.0 assists per game.
Wembanyama’s 22.1 points and 8.9 rebounds earned him a spot on the All‑NBA Second Team.

articles.title: Olympiakos clinches EuroLeague title in front of Giannis‑filled arena
articles.original_title: With Giannis in crowd, Olympiakos wins EuroLeague
articles.summary: Olympiakos defeated Real Madrid 92‑85 to capture the EuroLeague championship in Athens, with the crowd energized by the presence of NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo. The Greek club overcame a 12‑point early deficit to pull away in the second half.
key_points: Olympiakos out‑scored Madrid 55‑38 after trailing 15‑3 early.
Moustapha Fall led Olympiakos with 21 points and 9 rebounds.
Giannis attended the game, boosting fan enthusiasm but did not play.

articles.title: 2026 NBA mock draft predicts all 60 selections
articles.original_title: 🏀 2026 mock draft: Predicting all 60 picks
articles.summary: Analyst Jeremy Woo released a comprehensive mock draft following the 2026 NBA Combine, projecting every pick from first to second round. The draft features several high‑school prospects and international players reshaping team strategies.
key_points: Top three picks: 1. Victor Wembanyama (Detroit), 2. Scoot Henderson (Los Angeles Lakers), 3. Brandon Miller (Boston).
Lakers projected to select Scoot Henderson at #2, addressing their point‑guard need.
Woo highlights a surge in two‑way contract usage and early‑entry college seniors.

articles.title: Vlade Divac Explains Why LeBron James Isn’t in His Top 10 Players

key_points: Divac values long‑term franchise loyalty when evaluating greatness.
LeBron’s multiple team moves kept him out of Divac’s Top 10 despite his achievements.
The comment sparked discussion about how player movement influences legacy assessments.
